items.title: Coty exits Gucci license early
items.summary: Manila Times reports Coty agreed to hand the Gucci Beauty license back to Kering roughly a year early for about $400 million ($250 million upfront, $150 million due by September 2027, plus up to $30 million in performance incentives), with Coty continuing to operate the business through June 2027 before L'Oreal formally takes over. Framed for the Philippine business readership as confirmation the L'Oreal handover is accelerating.

items.title: Gucci unveils its latest high jewellery collection in Sydney
items.summary: Australian outlet RUSSH covers Gucci's High Jewellery collection presentation in Sydney, the line's first unveiling outside New York, spanning four chapters (Flora, Nodo, Everlasting G, Iconic Signatures) built on titanium, sapphires, rubies and the house's Horsebit and Marina Chain codes. Positions Sydney as a growing priority stop for Gucci's high-jewellery calendar.

items.title: Gucci's New High Jewellery Collection Is A Glittering Ode To Nature And House Heritage
items.summary: Marie Claire Australia's parallel writeup of the Sydney high-jewellery unveiling emphasizes craftsmanship storytelling -- sculptural titanium petals, gemstone gradation and articulated pieces -- as evidence Gucci is investing in high-margin jewellery categories to diversify away from leather goods amid the broader sales slump.

items.title: "Kenapa Gucci KLCC tutup" (Why did Gucci KLCC close)
items.summary: Malay-language Threads post questioning the closure of the Gucci boutique at Suria KLCC mall in Kuala Lumpur; drew 625 likes, 85 comments, 61 shares and roughly 129K views, with replies largely speculating about weak mall traffic post-pandemic. NOTE: published June 21, older than the 14-day freshness window -- retained only because it is the most substantive SEA critical/community signal found and no fresher equivalent surfaced; flagged as stale, weight accordingly.

items.title: Gucci KLCC permanently closed
items.summary: Malaysian tech/lifestyle forum thread on the Gucci KLCC closure; posters cite the mall's strict tenant-KPI policy (removal after sustained underperformance) and describe upper floors of Suria KLCC as increasingly quiet since the pandemic, with a skeptical, largely indifferent tone toward the brand's local relevance. NOTE: published June 22, older than the 14-day freshness window -- retained as the clearest SEA community-channel critical signal available; no fresher Malaysia/SEA store-closure discussion found this cycle.

items.title: Bangle watches are back (Gucci among revival houses)
items.summary: SCMP style roundup on the resurgence of bangle-style watches names Gucci among luxury houses reviving the format for 2026, part of a broader Asia-facing feature rather than a Gucci-specific story; included here for regional buzz volume tracking, not sentiment weight.

items.title: 2026 Gucci Flora
items.summary: Long-running PurseForum thread tracking the reissued Gucci Flora coated-canvas line under Demna (tote, bucket bag, Lady Lunetto, Gossip, GG Marmont pieces); ongoing member discussion mixes appreciation for the archival print revival with pointed comparisons to earlier Flora-era quality. EXCLUDED from item count as primary evidence due to age (started April) -- listed for context only, not counted toward today's pool; no July posts could be confirmed via public access.

collection_notes: Freshness was thin for SEA today: strong T1/T2 news coverage found (Coty/L'Oreal beauty-license follow-on, Sydney high-jewellery unveiling), but genuinely fresh (<=7 day) SNS/community items proved scarce despite dedicated searches (TikTok/Instagram/Threads/Reddit/PurseForum/Lowyat, English/Thai/Indonesian/Vietnamese/Korean). The two community/SNS items included (Threads + Lowyat, Gucci KLCC Malaysia store closure) are 19-20 days old, beyond the 14-day cutoff, but were kept and clearly flagged since they are the only substantive SEA-specific critical/community signal located; no fresher equivalent (positive or negative) was found on the public web this cycle. Critical-angle searches (price hikes, quality complaints, resale drop, boycott, overrated, store closures, earnings miss) surfaced the KLCC closure discussion and the broader APAC 22% revenue decline / Asia store-closure narrative (already covered in prior-day pools), but no new SEA-specific negative story published within the freshness window. Item 007 (PurseForum Flora thread) is excluded from the effective item count due to age; retained in file for transparency only.
